promptDef.name: user-list
promptDef.prompt:
# User List

The purpose of this application is to show a simple list of users from stubbed json file and present it using Tailwind and Angular framework.

## File system

You are in greenfield Angular starter app and here are the files


---

## Endpoints

You must use this endpoint in order to fetch the product detail model:
`GET https://storage.googleapis.com/lowgular-platform/mocks/users.json`

The endpoint will respond with the json looking similar to this type:

```ts
  {
    id: number,
    email: string,
    avatarUrl: string,
    lastLoggedInAt: number, // epoch timestamp since last login
  }[];
```

## Functional Requirements

```gherkin
Given the application fetches users data from the API endpoint
When I navigate to the "/" route
Then I should see the list of users
```

### Out of Functional Scope

The following are explicitly NOT part of this task so DO NOT attempt to develp these features:

- Error handling (assume the API always succeeds)
- Loading states (no need to show loading indicators)
- Empty state (no need to show info that list is empty)

## Technical Requirements

IMPORTANT: for this task you do not need to setup routing - simply use a single `App` component.

Use the simple CMS (Component Model Service) architecture:

- Component - Do not write your own component, simply use app.ts and app.html to finish the task
- Model - you are required to create own model file
- Service - you are required to create own service file
- HttpClient - you must use HttpClient to fetch data from the specified endpoint

## UI Requirements

You MUST create the valid HTML that will represent a list with items representing user data.

It is important that you bind the correct data into HTML tags.

You must bind the following product properties:

- avatarUrl (small rounded image)
- email (main text showing the user's email)
- lastLoggedInAt (tiny text showing last login in European format: day-month-year 24h time without second, e.g. "02.02.2025, 14:30")

IMPORTANT: The tailwind css is already added to the project so use its default utility classes.

We do not evaluate the visuals for this task, however as professional frontend developer you should take care that the app looks nice and is functional.

outputFiles.code:
User List

    @for (user of users(); track user.id) {

  • {{ user.email }}

    Last login: {{ user.lastLoggedInAt | europeanDate }}

    • }
/* Using Tailwind CSS - no additional styles needed */

User List

    @for (user of users(); track user.id) {

  • {{ user.email }}

    Last login: {{ user.lastLoggedInAt | europeanDate }}

    • }
/* Using Tailwind CSS - no additional styles needed */

assessments.name: Change Detection Strategy
assessments.description: Do not set the changeDetection property on @Component or @Directive; OnPush is the default. If you set it explicitly, use ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush and never Default.

assessments.name: Dependency Injection
assessments.description: Use the inject() function for dependency injection in components, directives, pipes, and injectable services. Do not use constructor parameters for injection.

assessments.name: Input and Output
assessments.description: Use input() and output() functions for component inputs and outputs. Do not use @Input() or @Output() decorators.
assessments.message: Analysis: found 0 properties related to input or output

assessments.name: No Mutate on Signals
assessments.description: For writable signals, use set() or update() to change values. Do not use mutate().
assessments.message: Analysis: no mutate() calls on signals found

assessments.name: No NgModules
assessments.description: Do not use NgModules. Use standalone components only.

assessments.name: Standalone Components
assessments.description: Do not set the standalone property on @Component, @Directive, or @Pipe (standalone is true by default). You may set standalone: true; never set standalone: false.

assessments.name: Pure State Transformations
assessments.description: Inside computed() and update() callbacks, keep transformations pure: no standalone function calls and no this.property = ... assignments.
assessments.message: Analysis: no update() or computed() callbacks found

assessments.name: Template Cognitive Load
assessments.description: Keep templates simple. Prefer flat conditionals; deep nesting of @if hurts readability. Use native control flow (@if, @for); avoid nesting conditionals more than two levels.

assessments.name: Template Properties
assessments.description: For component state used in templates, use signals (preferred) or RxJS observables. Do not use plain class properties for template-bound state.

assessments.name: No Derived State in Wrong Context
assessments.description: For derived state that reads signals, use computed(). Do not read signals inside property initializers or getters; that breaks reactivity.
assessments.message: Analysis: no signal reads in wrong context (property initializer or getter) found

assessments.name: Host Bindings
assessments.description: Do not use @HostBinding or @HostListener. Put host bindings and listeners in the host object of the @Component or @Directive decorator.

assessments.name: Class Binding
assessments.description: Use class bindings (e.g. [class.foo], [attr.class]) instead of ngClass in templates.

assessments.name: Style Binding
assessments.description: Use style bindings ([style.foo], [attr.style]) instead of ngStyle in templates.

assessments.name: Only Provided in Root
assessments.description: For singleton services, use providedIn: 'root' in the @Injectable decorator.

assessments.name: Form Binding
assessments.description: Prefer Reactive forms (formControl, formGroup, formArray) over template-driven forms (ngModel, ngForm, ngModelGroup).

assessments.name: Template Flow
assessments.description: Use native control flow (@if, @for, @switch) instead of *ngIf, *ngFor, *ngSwitch in templates.

assessments.name: Image Src Binding
assessments.description: Use NgOptimizedImage with ngSrc for img tags; do not use src or [attr.src] for static or bound image sources.

assessments.name: Observable to Signal
assessments.description: Prefer creating a signal from an RxJS Observable with toSignal() rather than subscribing and calling .set() inside the subscribe block.

assessments.name: No Any Type Definitions
assessments.description: Avoid the any type; use unknown when the type is uncertain. Prefer strict typing for properties, methods, and their signatures.

assessments.name: No Globals in Templates
assessments.description: Do not assume globals like new Date(), Math, window, or document are available in templates.

assessments.name: Prefer Inline Templates
assessments.description: Prefer inline templates for small components (under 20 lines). Use external HTML files only when the template is 20 or more lines.

assessments.name: Component Paths
assessments.description: When using external template or style files, use paths relative to the component TS file (e.g. ./app.html, ./app.css).

details.systemPromptGeneration:
Follow instructions below CAREFULLY:

- Code MUST be implemented in Angular.
- Put the component code inside `src/app/app.ts`
- Component class MUST always be named `App`
- Component's selector MUST always be "app-root" (the `selector: 'app-root'` MUST be present in the "@Component" decorator)
- Use standalone components, do NOT generate NgModules
- Generate the template code inside a separate HTML file and link it in the `template` field of the `App` component
- Generate the styling code inside a separate CSS file and link it in the `styleUrl` field of the `App` component
- Use Tailwind CSS
- Completeness: include all necessary code to run independently
- Use comments sparingly and only for complex parts of the code
- Make sure the generated code is **complete** and **runnable**
- Make sure the generated code contains a **complete** implementation of the `App` class
- Do NOT generate `bootstrapApplication` calls
- If the task requires to fetch data using `HttpClient` make sure to use `provideHttpClient()` in `bootstrapApplication` providers
- Do not assume globals like (`new Date()`) are available.
- Do not write arrow functions in templates (they are not supported).
- Do not write Regular expressions in templates (they are not supported).

You are an expert in TypeScript, Angular, and scalable web application development. You write functional, maintainable, performant, and accessible code following Angular and TypeScript best practices.

### TypeScript Best Practices

- Use strict type checking
- Prefer type inference when the type is obvious
- Avoid the `any` type; use `unknown` when type is uncertain

### Angular Best Practices

- Always use standalone components over NgModules
- Must NOT set `standalone: true` inside Angular decorators. It's the default in Angular v20+.
- Use signals for state management
- Implement lazy loading for feature routes
- Do NOT use the `@HostBinding` and `@HostListener` decorators. Put host bindings inside the `host` object of the `@Component` or `@Directive` decorator instead
- Use `NgOptimizedImage` for all static images.
  - `NgOptimizedImage` does not work for inline base64 images.

### Components

- Keep components small and focused on a single responsibility
- Use `input()` and `output()` functions instead of decorators
- Use `computed()` for derived state
- NEVER use `changeDetection` property in `@Component` decorator because by default it is set to `ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush`
- Prefer inline templates for small components
- Prefer Reactive forms instead of Template-driven ones
- Do NOT use `ngModel` directive
- Do NOT use `ngClass`, use `class` bindings instead
- DO NOT use `ngStyle`, use `style` bindings instead
- When using external templates/styles, use paths relative to the component TS file.
- Formatting HTML templates MUST prioritize using built-in pipes (e.g. currency, number, date) or create custom pure pipe. Do NOT bloat your components with pure methods that are only responsible for formatting data and return formatted value

### State Management

- Use signals for local component state
- Use `computed()` for derived state
- Keep state transformations pure and predictable
- Do NOT use `mutate` on signals, use `update` or `set` instead
- Prefer to create a signal properties from an RxJs Observable with `toSignal` and avoid setting values manually with `.set()` inside a subscribe block

### Templates

- Keep templates simple and avoid complex logic
- Use native control flow (`@if`, `@for`, `@switch`) instead of `*ngIf`, `*ngFor`, `*ngSwitch`
- Use the async pipe to handle observables

### Services

- Design services around a single responsibility
- Use the `providedIn: 'root'` option for singleton services
- ALWAYS use the `inject()` function and NEVER use constructor parameters for dependency injection
